222			Client:        mgr.GetClient(),
223			Scheme:        mgr.GetScheme(),
224			ClientSet:     clientset,
225			DynamicClient: dynamic.NewForConfigOrDie(config),
226			Recorder: events.NewKubeRecorder(
227				clientset.CoreV1().Events(utils.Namespace()),
228				utils.OperatorName,

242	func RunClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandControllers(mgr ctrl.Manager) {

256			unableToAddRunnable, runnableKey, "GlobalPullSecretSyncer")

259	func RunClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandWebHook(mgr ctrl.Manager) {
260		config := ctrl.GetConfigOrDie()
261		clientset := kubernetes.NewForConfigOrDie(config)
262		pool, err := ants.NewMultiPool(16, 16, ants.LeastTasks,
263			ants.WithPreAlloc(true))
264		must(err, "unable to create multi pool for the webhook's event messages")
265		postFuncs = append(postFuncs, func() {
266			err = pool.ReleaseTimeout(30 * time.Second)
267			if err != nil {
268				setupLog.Error(err, "failed to release the worker pool")
269			}
270			ants.Release()
271		})
272		handler := podplacement.NewPodSchedulingGateMutatingWebHook(mgr.GetClient(), clientset, mgr.GetScheme(),
273			mgr.GetEventRecorderFor(utils.OperatorName), pool) //nolint:staticcheck // MULTIARCH-6087: will be fixed with events API migration
274		mgr.GetWebhookServer().Register("/add-pod-scheduling-gate", &webhook.Admission{Handler: handler})
275	}

291	}

293	func validateFlags() error {
294		if !enableOperator && !enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandControllers && !enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandWebHook && !enableENoExecEventControllers {
295			return errors.New("at least one of the following flags must be set: --enable-operator, --enable-ppc-controllers, --enable-ppc-webhook, --enable-enoexec-event-controllers")
296		}
297		// no more than one of the flags can be set
298		if btoi(enableOperator)+btoi(enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandControllers)+btoi(enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandWebHook)+btoi(enableENoExecEventControllers) > 1 {
299			return errors.New("only one of the following flags can be set: --enable-operator, --enable-ppc-controllers, --enable-ppc-webhook, --enable-enoexec-event-controllers")
300		}
301		return nil
302	}
303	
304	func bindFlags() {
305		flag.StringVar(&metricsAddr, "metrics-bind-address", ":8080", "The address the metric endpoint binds to.")
306		flag.StringVar(&probeAddr, "health-probe-bind-address", ":8081", "The address the probe endpoint binds to.")
307		flag.BoolVar(&enableLeaderElection, "leader-elect", false,
308			"Enable leader election for controller manager. "+
309				"Enabling this will ensure there is only one active controller manager.")
310		flag.StringVar(&certDir, "cert-dir", "/var/run/manager/tls", "The directory where the TLS certs are stored")
311		// TODO: Change the defaults to match a local secret; the OCP specific settings will be provided by the operator
312		flag.StringVar(&globalPullSecretNamespace, "global-pull-secret-namespace", "openshift-config", "The namespace where the global pull secret is stored")
313		flag.StringVar(&globalPullSecretName, "global-pull-secret-name", "pull-secret", "The name of the global pull secret")
314		flag.StringVar(&registryCertificatesConfigMapName, "registry-certificates-configmap-name", "image-registry-certificates", "The name of the configmap that contains the registry certificates")
315		flag.BoolVar(&enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandWebHook, "enable-ppc-webhook", false, "Enable the pod placement config operand webhook")
316		flag.BoolVar(&enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandControllers, "enable-ppc-controllers", false, "Enable the pod placement config operand controllers")
317		flag.BoolVar(&enableOperator, "enable-operator", false, "Enable the operator")
318		flag.BoolVar(&enableCPPCInformer, "enable-cppc-informer", false, "Enable informer for ClusterPodPlacementConfig")
319		flag.BoolVar(&enableENoExecEventControllers, "enable-enoexec-event-controllers", false, "Enable the ENoExecEvent controllers")
320		// This may be deprecated in the future. It is used to support the current way of setting the log level for operands
321		// If operands will start to support a controller that watches the ClusterPodPlacementConfig, this flag may be removed
322		// and the log level will be set in the ClusterPodPlacementConfig at runtime (with no need for reconciliation)
323		flag.IntVar(&initialLogLevel, "initial-log-level", common.LogVerbosityLevelNormal.ToZapLevelInt(), "Initial log level. Converted to zap")
324		klog.InitFlags(nil)
325		flag.Parse()
326		// Set the Log Level as AtomicLevel to allow runtime changes
327		utils.AtomicLevel = zapuber.NewAtomicLevelAt(zapcore.Level(int8(-initialLogLevel))) // #nosec G115 -- initialLogLevel is constrained to 0-3 range
328		zapLogger := zap.New(zap.Level(utils.AtomicLevel), zap.UseDevMode(false))
329		klog.SetLogger(zapLogger)
330		ctrllog.SetLogger(zapLogger)
331	}

333	func must(err error, msg string, keysAndValues ...interface{}) {
334		if err != nil {
335			setupLog.Error(err, msg, keysAndValues...)
336			os.Exit(1)
337		}
338	}

343		}
344		return 0

271	func (r *ClusterPodPlacementConfigReconciler) ensureNamespaceLabels(ctx context.Context) error {
272		// https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/security/pod-security-standards/
273		// https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/security/pod-security-admission/
274		// https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/enforce-standards-namespace-labels/
275		log := ctrllog.FromContext(ctx)
276		log.V(1).Info("Ensuring namespace labels", "namespace", utils.Namespace())
277	
278		ns, err := r.ClientSet.CoreV1().Namespaces().Get(ctx, utils.Namespace(), metav1.GetOptions{})
279		if err != nil {
280			log.Error(err, "Unable to get the namespace")
281			return err
282		}
283		if ns.Labels == nil {
284			ns.Labels = make(map[string]string)
285		}
286		ns.Labels["pod-security.kubernetes.io/audit"] = "privileged"
287		ns.Labels["pod-security.kubernetes.io/audit-version"] = "v1.29"
288		ns.Labels["pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce"] = "privileged"
289		ns.Labels["pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce-version"] = "v1.29"
290		ns.Labels["pod-security.kubernetes.io/warn"] = "privileged"
291		ns.Labels["pod-security.kubernetes.io/warn-version"] = "v1.29"
292		// See https://github.com/openshift/enhancements/blob/c5b9aea25e/enhancements/workload-partitioning/management-workload-partitioning.md
293		ns.Labels["workload.openshift.io/allowed"] = "management"
294		log.V(2).Info("Updating the namespace labels", "labels", ns.Labels)
295		_, err = r.ClientSet.CoreV1().Namespaces().Update(ctx, ns, metav1.UpdateOptions{})
296		return err
297	}

299	// getCorrectHostmountAnyUIDSCC computes the SCC to use for the operator's wokrloads requiring hostPath mounts.
300	// OpenShift 4.19 introduced a new SCC `hostmount-anyuid-v2` with elevated privileges
301	// compared to `hostmount-anyuid`, allowing pods to mount specific hostPath volumes
302	// that may otherwise be restricted. For more details, see following bugs:
303	// https://issues.redhat.com/browse/OCPBUGS-55013
304	// https://issues.redhat.com/browse/MULTIARCH-5405
305	func (r *ClusterPodPlacementConfigReconciler) getCorrectHostmountAnyUIDSCC(ctx context.Context) (string, *corev1.SELinuxOptions, error) {
306		log := ctrllog.FromContext(ctx)
307		log.V(1).Info("Ensuring using the correct hostmount scc for podplacementconfig")
308	
309		minor, err := framework.GetClusterMinorVersion(r.ClientSet)
310		if err != nil {
311			return "", nil, err
312		}
313		// OpenShift version to Kubernetes version mapping assumption:
314		// - OpenShift 4.18 maps to Kubernetes 1.31.x
315		// - OpenShift 4.19 maps to Kubernetes 1.32.x and so on
316		// - Assume this mapping will remain stable after GA (Generally Available) release
317		// We use this check to decide which SCC (SecurityContextConstraint) to use
318		if minor < 30 {
319			// logic for Kubernetes < 1.30 (OpenShift < 4.17)
320			return "hostmount-anyuid", nil, nil
321		}
322	
323		// Because this MCO PR https://github.com/openshift/machine-config-operator/pull/4933
324		// was backported to OCP 4.17 and 4.18, it is causing a regression for MTO in those versions.
325		// Adding a temporary workaround here to give the pod temporary privileged SCC access.
326		if minor == 30 || minor == 31 {
327			return "privileged", &corev1.SELinuxOptions{
328				Type: "spc_t",
329			}, nil
330		}
331	
332		// logic for default set for Kubernetes >= 1.32 (OpenShift >= 4.19)
333		return "hostmount-anyuid-v2", &corev1.SELinuxOptions{
334			Type: "spc_t",
335		}, nil
336	}

338	// dependentsStatusToClusterPodPlacementConfig gathers the status of the dependents of the ClusterPodPlacementConfig object.
339	// The status is propagated to the ClusterPodPlacementConfig object.
340	func (r *ClusterPodPlacementConfigReconciler) dependentsStatusToClusterPodPlacementConfig(ctx context.Context, config *multiarchv1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfig) error {
341		log := ctrllog.FromContext(ctx).WithValues("ClusterPodPlacementConfig", config.Name,
342			"function", "updateStatus")
343		podPlacementController, err := r.ClientSet.AppsV1().Deployments(utils.Namespace()).Get(ctx, utils.PodPlacementControllerName, metav1.GetOptions{})
344		if client.IgnoreNotFound(err) != nil {
345			log.Error(err, "Unable to get the PodPlacement controller deployment")
346			return err
347		}
348	
349		if config.DeletionTimestamp.IsZero() && !podPlacementController.DeletionTimestamp.IsZero() {
350			// remove the finalizer in case the pod placement controller is deleted and we should reconcile it
351			log.Info("Removing the finalizer from the pod-placement-controller to allow reconciliation")
352			if controllerutil.RemoveFinalizer(podPlacementController, utils.PodPlacementFinalizerName) {
353				if err = r.Update(ctx, podPlacementController); err != nil {
354					log.Error(err, "Unable to remove the finalizer from the pod-placement-controller")
355					return err
356				}
357			}
358		}
359	
360		podPlacementWebhook, err := r.ClientSet.AppsV1().Deployments(utils.Namespace()).Get(ctx, utils.PodPlacementWebhookName, metav1.GetOptions{})
361		if client.IgnoreNotFound(err) != nil {
362			log.Error(err, "Unable to get the PodPlacement webhook deployment")
363			return err
364		}
365		_, err = r.ClientSet.AdmissionregistrationV1().MutatingWebhookConfigurations().Get(ctx, utils.PodMutatingWebhookConfigurationName, metav1.GetOptions{})
366		if client.IgnoreNotFound(err) != nil {
367			log.Error(err, "Unable to get the mutating webhook configuration")
368			return err
369		}
370		config.Status.Build(
371			isDeploymentAvailable(podPlacementController), isDeploymentAvailable(podPlacementWebhook),
372			isDeploymentUpToDate(podPlacementController), isDeploymentUpToDate(podPlacementWebhook),
373			// err == nil means the MutatingWebhookConfiguration is available
374			err == nil, !config.DeletionTimestamp.IsZero())
375		return nil
376	}

378	// handleDelete handles the deletion of the PodPlacement operand's resources.
379	func (r *ClusterPodPlacementConfigReconciler) handleDelete(ctx context.Context,

381		// The ClusterPodPlacementConfig is being deleted, cleanup the resources
382		log := ctrllog.FromContext(ctx).WithValues("operation", "handleDelete")
383	
384		err := r.handleEnoexecDelete(ctx, clusterPodPlacementConfig)
385		if err != nil {
386			return err
387		}
388	
389		// The error by the updateStatus function, if any, is ignored, as the deletion should always proceed.
390		// We execute the update here because this function returns multiple times before the whole deletion process is completed.
391		// Executing it here ensures that the conditions are updated throughout the deletion process.
392		_ = r.updateStatus(ctx, clusterPodPlacementConfig)
393	
394		// Prevent deletion while PodPlacementConfig resources still exist
395		// Check this BEFORE tearing down webhook/operand resources to prevent orphaning PPCs
396		if controllerutil.ContainsFinalizer(clusterPodPlacementConfig, utils.CPPCNoPPCObjectFinalizer) {
397			log.V(1).Info("Checking for existing PodPlacementConfig resources before deletion")
398			ppcList := &multiarchv1beta1.PodPlacementConfigList{}
399			if err := r.List(ctx, ppcList); err != nil {
400				log.Error(err, "Unable to list PodPlacementConfigs during deletion")
401				return err
402			}
403			if len(ppcList.Items) > 0 {
404				// Block deletion - PPCs still exist
405				err := errors.New(RemainingPodPlacementConfig)
406				log.Error(err, "Deletion blocked due to existing PodPlacementConfig resources")

409			// All PPCs are gone, remove the finalizer
410			log.V(1).Info("No PodPlacementConfig resources found, removing no-pod-placement-config finalizer")
411			controllerutil.RemoveFinalizer(clusterPodPlacementConfig, utils.CPPCNoPPCObjectFinalizer)
412			if err := r.Update(ctx, clusterPodPlacementConfig); err != nil {

567	func (r *ClusterPodPlacementConfigReconciler) handleEnoexecDelete(ctx context.Context, clusterPodPlacementConfig *multiarchv1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfig) error {
568		var err error
569		log := ctrllog.FromContext(ctx, "operation", "handleEnoexecDelete")
570		// If the ENoExecEvent finalizer was never added, skip the entire cleanup process.
571		// This prevents attempting to list ENoExecEvent resources when the plugin was never enabled,
572		// which would cause the informer to be created and fail to sync in time.
573		if !controllerutil.ContainsFinalizer(clusterPodPlacementConfig, utils.ExecFormatErrorFinalizerName) {
574			log.V(1).Info("ENoExecEvent finalizer not present, skipping ENoExecEvent cleanup")
575			return nil
576		}
577		daemonSetRelatedObjsToDelete := []utils.ToDeleteRef{
578			{
579				NamespacedTypedClient: r.ClientSet.RbacV1().ClusterRoles(),
580				ObjName:               utils.EnoexecDaemonSet,
581			},
582			{
583				NamespacedTypedClient: r.ClientSet.RbacV1().ClusterRoleBindings(),
584				ObjName:               utils.EnoexecDaemonSet,
585			},
586			{
587				NamespacedTypedClient: r.ClientSet.RbacV1().Roles(utils.Namespace()),
588				ObjName:               utils.EnoexecDaemonSet,
589			},
590			{
591				NamespacedTypedClient: r.ClientSet.RbacV1().RoleBindings(utils.Namespace()),
592				ObjName:               utils.EnoexecDaemonSet,
593			},
594			{
595				NamespacedTypedClient: r.ClientSet.CoreV1().ServiceAccounts(utils.Namespace()),
596				ObjName:               utils.EnoexecDaemonSet,
597			},
598			{
599				NamespacedTypedClient: r.ClientSet.AppsV1().DaemonSets(utils.Namespace()),
600				ObjName:               utils.EnoexecDaemonSet,
601			},
602		}
603		log.Info("Deleting the DaemonSet ENoExecEvent resources")
604		if err := utils.DeleteResources(ctx, daemonSetRelatedObjsToDelete); err != nil {
605			log.Error(err, "Unable to delete DaemonSet resources")
606			return err
607		}
608		ds := &appsv1.DaemonSet{}
609		err = r.Get(ctx, client.ObjectKey{
610			Name:      utils.EnoexecDaemonSet,
611			Namespace: utils.Namespace(),
612		}, ds)
613		if err == nil {
614			log.Info("Waiting for the eNoExecEvent DaemonSet to be fully deleted...")
615			// Return an error to force requeue. This is a common pattern for waiting.
616			return newRequeueAfterError(5*time.Second, "enoexec DaemonSet is still terminating")
617		}
618		if client.IgnoreNotFound(err) != nil {
619			log.Error(err, "Failed to check for the eNoExecEvent DaemonSet status")
620			return err
621		}
622		log.Info("eNoExecEvent DaemonSet has been deleted. Proceeding with eNoExecEvent controller cleanup.")
623		enoexecEventList := &multiarchv1beta1.ENoExecEventList{}
624		err = r.List(ctx, enoexecEventList, client.InNamespace(utils.Namespace()))

627			return err
628		}
629	
630		// Delete errored ENoExecEvent resources and count remaining non-errored ones
631		nonErroredCount, _ := r.deleteErroredENoExecEvents(ctx, enoexecEventList)
632	
633		// Only block cleanup if there are non-errored ENoExecEvent resources.
634		// The enoexec-controller deployment is still running at this point so it can
635		// process the remaining events before we proceed.
636		if nonErroredCount > 0 {
637			log.Info("Found existing non-errored ENoExecEvent resources, waiting for reconciliation", "nonErroredCount", nonErroredCount)
638			return newRequeueAfterError(5*time.Second, "found existing ENoExecEvent resources that need reconciliation")
639		}
640	
641		log.Info("No non-errored ENoExecEvent resources found in the cluster. Removing the ExecEvent finalizer from the ENoExec Deployment")
642		deployment := &appsv1.Deployment{}
643		err = r.Get(ctx, client.ObjectKey{
644			Name:      utils.EnoexecControllerName,
645			Namespace: utils.Namespace(),
646		}, deployment)
647		if client.IgnoreNotFound(err) != nil {
648			log.Error(err, "Could not fetch ENoExecEvent Deployment")
649			return err
650		}
651		if err == nil && controllerutil.RemoveFinalizer(deployment, utils.ExecFormatErrorFinalizerName) {
652			if err = r.Update(ctx, deployment); err != nil {
653				log.Error(err, "Unable to remove finalizers.",
654					deployment.Kind, deployment.Name)
655				return err
656			}
657		}
658	
659		deploymentRelatedObjsToDelete := []utils.ToDeleteRef{
660			{
661				NamespacedTypedClient: r.ClientSet.AppsV1().Deployments(utils.Namespace()),
662				ObjName:               utils.EnoexecControllerName,
663			},
664			{
665				NamespacedTypedClient: r.ClientSet.RbacV1().Roles(utils.Namespace()),
666				ObjName:               utils.EnoexecControllerName,
667			},
668			{
669				NamespacedTypedClient: r.ClientSet.RbacV1().RoleBindings(utils.Namespace()),
670				ObjName:               utils.EnoexecControllerName,
671			},
672			{
673				NamespacedTypedClient: r.ClientSet.RbacV1().ClusterRoles(),
674				ObjName:               utils.EnoexecControllerName,
675			},
676			{
677				NamespacedTypedClient: r.ClientSet.RbacV1().ClusterRoleBindings(),

679			},
680			{
681				NamespacedTypedClient: r.ClientSet.CoreV1().ServiceAccounts(utils.Namespace()),
682				ObjName:               utils.EnoexecControllerName,
683			},

688			{

690				ObjName:               utils.EnoexecControllerName,
691			},
692		}

694		if utils.IsResourceAvailable(ctx, r.DynamicClient, monitoringv1.SchemeGroupVersion.WithResource("servicemonitors")) {
695			deploymentRelatedObjsToDelete = append(deploymentRelatedObjsToDelete, utils.ToDeleteRef{
696				NamespacedTypedClient: utils.NewDynamicDeleter(r.DynamicClient.Resource(schema.GroupVersionResource{Group: "monitoring.coreos.com", Version: "v1", Resource: "servicemonitors"}).Namespace(utils.Namespace())),
697				ObjName:               utils.EnoexecControllerName,
698			}, utils.ToDeleteRef{
699				NamespacedTypedClient: utils.NewDynamicDeleter(r.DynamicClient.Resource(schema.GroupVersionResource{Group: "monitoring.coreos.com", Version: "v1", Resource: "prometheusrules"}).Namespace(utils.Namespace())),

703				ObjName:               utils.ExecFormatErrorsDetected,
704			})
705		}

707		log.Info("Deleting the ENoExecEvent Deployment resources")
708		if err = utils.DeleteResources(ctx, deploymentRelatedObjsToDelete); err != nil {
709			log.Error(err, "Unable to delete deployment resources")

713		if controllerutil.RemoveFinalizer(clusterPodPlacementConfig, utils.ExecFormatErrorFinalizerName) {

724	func (r *ClusterPodPlacementConfigReconciler) reconcile(ctx context.Context, clusterPodPlacementConfig *multiarchv1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfig) error {
725		log := ctrllog.FromContext(ctx)
726		desiredLogLevel := int8(-clusterPodPlacementConfig.Spec.LogVerbosity.ToZapLevelInt()) // #nosec G115 -- LogVerbosity values are constrained to 0-3 by enum
727		if int8(utils.AtomicLevel.Level()) != desiredLogLevel {
728			log.Info("Setting log level", "level", desiredLogLevel)
729			utils.AtomicLevel.SetLevel(zapcore.Level(desiredLogLevel))
730		}
731		if err := r.ensureNamespaceLabels(ctx); err != nil {
732			log.Error(err, "Unable to ensure namespace labels")
733			return errorutils.NewAggregate([]error{err, r.updateStatus(ctx, clusterPodPlacementConfig)})
734		}
735		clusterPodPlacementConfigObjects, err := r.buildPodPlacementConfigObjects(clusterPodPlacementConfig, ctx)
736		if err != nil {
737			return err
738		}
739	
740		execFormatErrorObjects := []client.Object{}
741		if clusterPodPlacementConfig.PluginsEnabled(common.ExecFormatErrorMonitorPluginName) {
742			execFormatErrorObjects, err = r.buildENoExecEventObjects(ctx, clusterPodPlacementConfig)
743			if err != nil {
744				return err
745			}
746		} else {
747			err := r.handleEnoexecDelete(ctx, clusterPodPlacementConfig)
748			if err != nil {
749				return err
750			}
751		}
752	
753		objects := append(clusterPodPlacementConfigObjects, execFormatErrorObjects...)
754	
755		// We ensure the MutatingWebHookConfiguration is created and present only if the operand is ready to serve the admission request and add/remove the scheduling gate.
756		shouldEnsureMWC := clusterPodPlacementConfig.Status.CanDeployMutatingWebhook()
757		shouldDeleteMWC := !shouldEnsureMWC && !clusterPodPlacementConfig.Status.IsMutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable()
758		if shouldEnsureMWC {
759			objects = append(objects, buildMutatingWebhookConfiguration(clusterPodPlacementConfig))
760		}
761		if shouldDeleteMWC {
762			log.Info("Deleting the mutating webhook configuration as the operand is not ready to serve the admission request or remove the scheduling gate")
763			_ = r.ClientSet.AdmissionregistrationV1().MutatingWebhookConfigurations().Delete(ctx, utils.PodMutatingWebhookConfigurationName, metav1.DeleteOptions{})
764		}
765	
766		// If the servicemonitors.monitoring.coreos.com CRD is available, we create the ServiceMonitor objects
767		if utils.IsResourceAvailable(ctx, r.DynamicClient, monitoringv1.SchemeGroupVersion.WithResource("servicemonitors")) {
768			log.V(1).Info("Creating ServiceMonitors")
769			objects = append(objects,
770				buildServiceMonitor(utils.PodPlacementControllerName),
771				buildServiceMonitor(utils.PodPlacementWebhookName),
772				buildCPPCAvailabilityAlertRule(),
773			)
774		} else {
775			log.V(1).Info("servicemonitoring.monitoring.coreos.com is not available. Skipping the creation of the ServiceMonitors")
776		}
777		errs := make([]error, 0)
778		for _, o := range objects {
779			if err := ctrl.SetControllerReference(clusterPodPlacementConfig, o, r.Scheme); err != nil {
780				log.Error(err, "Unable to set controller reference", "name", o.GetName())
781				errs = append(errs, err)
782			}
783		}
784	
785		if len(errs) > 0 {
786			return errorutils.NewAggregate(append(errs, r.updateStatus(ctx, clusterPodPlacementConfig)))
787		}
788	
789		if err := utils.ApplyResources(ctx, r.ClientSet, r.DynamicClient, r.Recorder, objects); err != nil {
790			log.Error(err, "Unable to apply resources")
791			return errorutils.NewAggregate([]error{err, r.updateStatus(ctx, clusterPodPlacementConfig)})
792		}
793	
794		return r.updateStatus(ctx, clusterPodPlacementConfig)
795	}

815			return err
816		}

818		if progressing {

63	// Reconcile will reconcile the ENoExecEvent resource.
64	// It will fetch the ENoExecEvent instance, retrieve the pod and node information,
65	// label the pod with the ENoExecEvent label, update the metrics, and publish an event.
66	// Finally, it will delete the ENoExecEvent resource if the reconciliation was successful or if the pod was not found.
67	func (r *Reconciler) Reconcile(ctx context.Context, req ctrl.Request) (ctrl.Result, error) {
68		logger := log.FromContext(ctx)
69		metrics.InitMetrics()
70		// Fetch the ENoExecEvent instance
71		enoExecEventObj := &multiarchv1beta1.ENoExecEvent{}
72		if err := r.Get(ctx, req.NamespacedName, enoExecEventObj); err != nil {
73			// If the resource is not found, we simply return. This is not an error.
74			return ctrl.Result{}, client.IgnoreNotFound(err)
75		}
76	
77		eNoExecEvent := NewENoExecEvent(enoExecEventObj, ctx, r.recorder)
78	
79		if eNoExecEvent.Namespace != utils.Namespace() {
80			logger.Info("ENoExecEvent is not in the operator namespace, skipping reconciliation", "name", eNoExecEvent.Name, "namespace", eNoExecEvent.Namespace)
81			r.markAsError(ctx, eNoExecEvent, ErrorReasonWrongNamespace)
82			// Return nil to avoid requeuing - this event will be cleaned up by deleteErroredENoExecEvents
83			return ctrl.Result{}, nil
84		}
85	
86		if eNoExecEvent.Status.PodName == "" {
87			// If the ENoExecEvent does not have a pod name, we ignore it
88			logger.V(5).Info("ENoExecEvent does not have a pod name, skipping reconciliation", "name", eNoExecEvent.Name, "namespace", eNoExecEvent.Namespace)
89			r.markAsError(ctx, eNoExecEvent, ErrorReasonPodNotFound)
90			// Return nil to avoid requeuing - this event will be cleaned up by deleteErroredENoExecEvents
91			return ctrl.Result{}, nil
92		}
93	
94		// Log the ENoExecEvent instance
95		logger.Info("Reconciling ENoExecEvent", "name", eNoExecEvent.Name, "namespace", eNoExecEvent.Namespace)
96		ret, err := r.reconcile(ctx, eNoExecEvent)
97		// If the reconciliation was successful, or one of the objects was not found, we delete the ENoExecEvent resource.
98		if client.IgnoreNotFound(err) == nil {
99			if err := r.Delete(ctx, &eNoExecEvent.ENoExecEvent); err != nil {
100				logger.Error(err, "Failed to delete ENoExecEvent resource after reconciliation", "name", eNoExecEvent.Name)
101				// Mark as error but return the original error so client.IgnoreNotFound works
102				r.markAsError(ctx, eNoExecEvent, ErrorReasonReconciliation)
103				return ctrl.Result{}, client.IgnoreNotFound(err)
104			}
105			metrics.EnoexecCounter.Inc()
106			logger.Info("Deleted ENoExecEvent resource after successful reconciliation", "name", eNoExecEvent.Name)
107			return ret, nil
108		}
109		metrics.EnoexecCounterInvalid.Inc()
110		logger.Error(err, "Failed to reconcile ENoExecEvent", "name", eNoExecEvent.Name)
111		// Mark as error - this is a real reconciliation error that will be retried
112		r.markAsError(ctx, eNoExecEvent, ErrorReasonReconciliation)
113		return ctrl.Result{}, err
114	}

116	func (r *Reconciler) reconcile(ctx context.Context, eNoExecEvent *ENoExecEvent) (ctrl.Result, error) {
117		logger := log.FromContext(ctx)
118		// Retrieve the pod
119		podObj, err := r.clientSet.CoreV1().Pods(eNoExecEvent.Status.PodNamespace).Get(ctx, eNoExecEvent.Status.PodName, metav1.GetOptions{})
120		if err != nil {
121			// If the pod is not found, the error will be ignored and the ENoExecEvent will be deleted by the caller.
122			// TODO: increment counter for exec format error failures and missed ENoExecEvent reconciliations
123			logger.Error(err, "Failed to get pod for ENoExecEvent", "podName",
124				eNoExecEvent.Status.PodName, "namespace", eNoExecEvent.Status.PodNamespace)
125			// Mark as error but return the original error so client.IgnoreNotFound works
126			r.markAsError(ctx, eNoExecEvent, ErrorReasonPodNotFound)
127			return ctrl.Result{}, err
128		}
129	
130		pod := models.NewPod(podObj, ctx, r.recorder)
131		if pod.PodObject().Spec.NodeName != eNoExecEvent.Status.NodeName {
132			// If the pod is not scheduled on the node where the ENoExecEvent was generated, we skip the reconciliation.
133			logger.Info("Pod is not scheduled on the node where the ENoExecEvent was generated", "podName",
134				pod.Name, "namespace", pod.Namespace, "nodeName", eNoExecEvent.Status.NodeName)
135			// This is not an error case - mark it and return nil so it gets deleted
136			r.markAsError(ctx, eNoExecEvent, ErrorReasonNodeNotFound)
137			return ctrl.Result{}, nil
138		}
139	
140		// Retrieve the node
141		node, err := r.clientSet.CoreV1().Nodes().Get(ctx, eNoExecEvent.Status.NodeName, metav1.GetOptions{})
142		if err != nil {
143			// When the error is "not found", the ENoExecEvent may refer a new pod with the same name scheduled on a different node.
144			// This ENoExecEvent is then not relevant anymore and will be deleted by the caller.
145			// Other errors will be returned to the caller, which will retry the reconciliation.
146			// TODO: increment counter for exec format error failures and missed ENoExecEvent reconciliations
147			logger.Error(err, "Failed to get node for ENoExecEvent", "nodeName", podObj.Spec.NodeName,
148				"podName", eNoExecEvent.Status.PodName, "namespace", eNoExecEvent.Status.PodNamespace)
149			// Mark as error but return the original error so client.IgnoreNotFound works
150			r.markAsError(ctx, eNoExecEvent, ErrorReasonNodeNotFound)
151			return ctrl.Result{}, err
152		}
153	
154		containerName, err := pod.ContainerNameFor(eNoExecEvent.Status.ContainerID)
155		if err != nil {
156			logger.Error(err, "Container ID not found in pod status", "containerID", eNoExecEvent.Status.ContainerID)
157			containerName = utils.UnknownContainer
158		}
159	
160		logger.Info("Publishing event for ENoExecEvent", "podName", pod.Name, "namespace", pod.Namespace)
161		pod.PublishEvent(v1.EventTypeWarning, utils.ExecFormatErrorEventReason,
162			utils.ExecFormatErrorEventMessage(containerName, node.Labels[utils.ArchLabel]))
163	
164		// Label the pod with the ENoExecEvent label.
165		pod.EnsureLabel(utils.ExecFormatErrorLabelKey, utils.True)
166		// TODO: increment counter for exec format error failures
167		// Update the pod with the new label
168		if _, err = r.clientSet.CoreV1().Pods(pod.Namespace).Update(ctx, pod.PodObject(), metav1.UpdateOptions{}); err != nil {
169			logger.Error(err, "Failed to label the pod", "podName", pod.Name, "namespace", pod.Namespace)
170			// if the error is "not found", it means the pod has been deleted, the caller will handle this case.
171			// Mark as error but return the original error so client.IgnoreNotFound works
172			r.markAsError(ctx, eNoExecEvent, ErrorReasonPodNotFound)
173			return ctrl.Result{}, err
174		}
175		return ctrl.Result{}, nil
176	}

179	func (r *Reconciler) SetupWithManager(mgr ctrl.Manager) error {
180		// This reconciler is mostly I/O bound due to the pod and node retrievals, so we can increase the number of concurrent
181		// reconciles to the number of CPUs * 4.
182		maxConcurrentReconciles := runtime2.NumCPU() * 4
183		log.FromContext(context.Background()).Info("Setting up the ENoExecEventReconciler with the manager with max"+
184			" concurrent reconciles", "maxConcurrentReconciles", maxConcurrentReconciles)
185	
186		return ctrl.NewControllerManagedBy(mgr).
187			For(&multiarchv1beta1.ENoExecEvent{}).
188			WithOptions(ctrl2.Options{
189				MaxConcurrentReconciles: maxConcurrentReconciles,
190			}).
191			Complete(r)
192	}
